violations.description: General employee practices. — --Food employee observed wearing jewelry on arms and/or hands during food preparation. Recommend removing watches and rings during food prep times.
General food protection. — --Potentially hazardous food was not stored at proper temperature of 41 degrees F or below. Container of steaks in hand sink at 49 degrees F.
Preventing contamination by employees. — --Employee observed handling ready-to-eat food with bare hands. Observed employees plating food items with bare hands. Use single service gloves or utensils.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Grease pooling under grills and build up around fryer.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Hood system needs cleaning. Build-up of grease and char.
Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. Container of raw steaks on hand sink. Keep hand sink clear to encourage proper hand washing.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. Towel dispenser at hand sink not working.
